text:
• Connection Establishment Rate Limiting Policy Cheat Sheet
What It Is
---
Architecture
An adaptive rate limiter for incoming TCP connections that prevents connection storms from overwhelming MongoDB during high CPU load.
Connection Establishment Rate Limiting Policy
POLL
DECIDE
TUNE
CPU idles
from /proc
Connection
rate from
serverstat
levate
onn rate
Multiply rate
by scalar
× 0.95 (down)
× 1.05-1.30
(up)
MongoDB Parameters Modified
Parameter
Type
ingressConnectionEstablishmentRateLimiterEnabled
bool
ingressConnectionEstablishmentRatePerSec
u32
ingressConnectionEstablishmentBurstCapacitySecs
f32
ingressConnectionEstablishmentMaxQueueDepth
u32
Description
Master switch
Connections/sec allowed
Burst window (seconds)
Max queued connections
Signals Monitored
Signal
Source
CPU Idle %
/proc/stat
Connection Rate
serverStatus connections.totalCreated
Rejected Connections
serverStatus connections.establishmentRateLimit.rejected
Queued Connections
serverStatus queues. ingressSessionEstablishment.addedToQueue
What It Measures
System CPU availability
New connections/sec
Connections dropped by limiter
Connections waiting
Thresholds & Classification
CPU Overload Levels
Level
Condition
Default
None
idle ≥ 2 × threshold
idle ≥ 20%
Mild
threshold ≤ idle < 2 × threshold
10% s idle C 20%
Severe
idle < threshold
idle < 10%
Connection Rate Levels
Based on conn_rate_core multiplier x num_cores (default multiplier: 4.0):
Level
Formula
Example (8 cores)
None
rate ≤ 0.5 x multiplier x cores
≤ 16 conn/sec
Low
rate > 0.5 x multiplier x cores
> 16 conn/sec
Medium
rate > multiplier × cores
> 32 conn/sec
High
rate > 2 × multiplier × cores
> 64 conn/sec
Decision Rules
DECREASE rate limit when:
CPU Overload = Severe (idle < 10%)
AND
Connection Rate # None (any elevated rate)
INCREASE rate limit when:
Rate
limiting detected (connections rejected
or queued)
AND
CPU Overload = None (idle ≥ 20%)
Note: Changes only happen after stabilization_ interval_secs (default: 5s) since last change.
